intro: Hymenochlaena palawanensis, also known as Palawan Hymenochlaena, is a medicinal plant species native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Hymenochlaena palawanensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and it can also be found in various herbal supplements and remedies, such as teas, infusions, and capsules.
